patch.value: GH-542: MCP pm_copy nests title/message under options while sibling tools declare flat camelCase params — own suite triggers unexpected-arg warnings

pm_copy inputSchema declares title/message (and idOnly if supported) as flat top-level params derived from COPY_FLAG_CONTRACTS; options stays accepted for back-compat; Contract test asserts every narrow tool's declared top-level keys cover its action's flag contract (options-vs-flat drift fails fast); MCP integration suites run warning-clean: pm_close 'text' arg fixed and zero unexpected-top-level-argument warnings asserted

The MCP boundary's declared-keys warning (shipped by pm-qxwu via pm-at83) is only trustworthy if each tool's declared surface matches what the runtime actually accepts. pm_copy under-declares: runtime handler spreads args into runAction so flat title/message work, but the schema routes them through nested options — the ONLY narrow mutation tool shaped this way. Evidence in-repo: tests/integration/mcp-dynamic-package-actions.spec.ts ~:1340 passes flat title/message/idOnly and every CI run emits 3 warnings; same suite passes 'text' to pm_close (not a real param, silently ignored — assertion value untested). Class framing: this is the INPUT-side sibling of pm-srns (uniform output contract) and belongs to the mpbb declared-contract-rendering ledger family (6y58/9qcr/ulqu render FROM contract domain; this one DECLARES from contract). Fix in tool-definitions.ts by deriving from COPY_FLAG_CONTRACTS, not by hand-adding keys.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Scope extension (pass #77, 2026-07-13): the fenced ignore block MUST also cover .agents/pm/locks/ — concrete failure: review pass #76's commit (67ccbcf4) accidentally committed .agents/pm/locks/reindex.lock (a live-pid runtime lock) because this repo's .gitignore covered runtime/ and search/ but not locks/. The tracked-cache health warning (AC half 3) should likewise flag tracked lock files. Our own repo's .gitignore gets the locks/ line directly this pass; pm init's fenced block owns it for user repos.
